title: Incomplete credentialing files for three locum tenens physicians
description: During review of temporary privileging files, three locum tenens physicians were found to have incomplete credentialing documentation. Missing items included current DEA certificates, malpractice history attestations, and peer references.

title: Hand hygiene compliance below threshold in ICU
description: Q3 hand hygiene surveillance data showed 71% compliance in the Medical ICU, below the 85% organizational threshold. Failures were concentrated during shift change windows.

title: QAPI meeting minutes lack documented follow-through on action items
description: Review of QAPI committee meeting minutes from Q2 and Q3 revealed that action items from previous meetings were not consistently tracked to closure. No standardized mechanism exists for follow-up accountability.

title: Medication reconciliation not completed within 24 hours of admission
description: Chart audit of 50 admissions at Dillon facility revealed 14 cases (28%) where medication reconciliation was not completed within 24 hours of admission. Delays ranged from 30 to 72 hours.

title: Patient reassessment documentation gaps in post-surgical unit
description: Tracer activity in the Darlington post-surgical unit identified that 6 of 20 charts reviewed lacked documented reassessments at the intervals specified by hospital policy.

title: Hand hygiene compliance below threshold in ED — Dillon
description: Q3 hand hygiene audit at Dillon ED showed 68% compliance against 85% threshold. Audit noted that wall-mounted sanitizer dispensers near triage were frequently empty.

title: Governing body review of quality data not documented for Q2
description: Board meeting minutes from Q2 2025 do not include documented evidence that quality and patient safety data were formally reviewed by the governing body as required.

title: Infection prevention policy not updated to reflect current CDC guidance
description: The current infection prevention policy references 2021 CDC isolation precaution guidance. Updated 2024 CDC guidance has not been incorporated.
